title: The sphere packing problem in dimension 8

thm:1.summary: $E_8$ is the densest sphere packing in $\mathbb{R}^8$: no packing of unit balls in $\mathbb{R}^8$ has density greater than that of the $E_8$-lattice packing — the Fields-medal result resolving Conjecture 2 of Cohn–Elkies for $n=8$.

thm:2.summary: Restatement of the Cohn–Elkies linear-programming bound: an admissible $f:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}$ with $f(x)\le 0$ for $\|x\|\ge 1$, $\widehat{f}(x)\ge 0$ everywhere, and $f(0)=\widehat{f}(0)$ gives a density bound. Imported from Cohn–Elkies (math/0110009).

thm:3.summary: Existence of the magic function for $E_8$: a radial Schwartz $g:\mathbb{R}^8\to\mathbb{R}$ with $g(x)\le 0$ for $\|x\|\ge\sqrt{2}$, $\widehat{g}(x)\ge 0$ everywhere, and $g(0)=\widehat{g}(0)=1$ — the function whose existence, by Theorem 2 applied at radius $r=\sqrt{2}$, proves Theorem 1.

thm:4.summary: Explicit construction of the magic function: $g(x):=\frac{\pi i}{8640}a(x)+\frac{i}{240\pi}b(x)$ satisfies the three magic-function conditions, and $g$ and $\widehat{g}$ vanish only at vectors $x$ with $\|x\|^2\in 2\mathbb{Z}_{>0}$ — exactly the squared lengths of $E_8$ vectors.

prop:1.summary: The function $a$ is a Schwartz $+1$-eigenfunction of the Fourier transform: $\widehat{a}(x)=a(x)$ — the symmetric ingredient of the magic-function combination.

prop:2.summary: Modular-form integral representation of $a$ for $r>\sqrt{2}$: $a(r) = -4\sin^2(\pi r^2/2)\int_0^{i\infty}\phi_0(-1/z)\,z^2\,e^{\pi i r^2 z}\,dz$ — expresses $a$ as a contour integral of a quasi-modular form.

prop:3.summary: Explicit formula for $a(r)$, $r\ge 0$: combines a rational part with poles at $r=0,\sqrt{2}$ and an absolutely convergent integral — the form used to read off $a(0)$, $a(\sqrt{2})$, and $a'(\sqrt{2})$.

prop:4.summary: Boundary values of $a$: $a(0)=-i\,8640/\pi$, $a(\sqrt{2})=0$, $a'(\sqrt{2})=i\,72\sqrt{2}/\pi$ — the values calibrating the $\pi i/8640$ coefficient in $g$'s definition.

prop:5.summary: The function $b$ is a Schwartz $-1$-eigenfunction of the Fourier transform: $\widehat{b}(x)=-b(x)$ — the antisymmetric ingredient combining with $a$ to form $g$.

prop:6.summary: Modular-form integral representation of $b$ for $r>\sqrt{2}$: $b(r) = -4\sin^2(\pi r^2/2)\int_0^{i\infty}\psi_I(z)\,e^{\pi i r^2 z}\,dz$.

prop:7.summary: Explicit formula for $b(r)$, $r\ge 0$: a rational part with poles at $r=0,\sqrt{2}$ plus an absolutely convergent integral — the form used to read off $b$'s values.

prop:8.summary: Boundary values of $b$: $b(0)=0$, $b(\sqrt{2})=0$, $b'(\sqrt{2})=2\sqrt{2}\,\pi i$ — the values calibrating the $i/(240\pi)$ coefficient in $g$'s definition.

edges.note: Applying the Cohn–Elkies LP bound (Theorem 2) at radius $r=\sqrt{2}$ with the magic function from Theorem 3 yields the density bound met by $E_8$.
The proof uses the Cohn–Elkies LP bound (Theorem 2).
Existence of the magic function is established by the explicit formula in Theorem 4.
The coefficient $\pi i/8640$ is determined by $a(0)$ from Proposition 4.
The coefficient $i/(240\pi)$ is determined by $b'(\sqrt{2})$ from Proposition 8.
The modular-form integral representation derives from the definition of $a$ used in Proposition 1.
The explicit formula on $[0,\sqrt{2}]$ is obtained by analytic continuation from the $r>\sqrt{2}$ integral representation.
Boundary values are read off from the explicit formula.
The modular-form integral representation derives from the definition of $b$.
The explicit formula is the analytic continuation of the integral representation.
Boundary values are read off from the explicit formula.

main_result.description: The headline: $E_8$ is the densest packing in $\mathbb{R}^8$.

LP_bound_import.description: The Cohn–Elkies linear-programming bound, restated from math/0110009 as the framework Viazovska's construction plugs into.

magic_function.description: Existence and explicit form of the magic function $g$ for $E_8$, written as $g = \frac{\pi i}{8640}a + \frac{i}{240\pi}b$.

eigenfunction_a.description: The $+1$-Fourier-eigenfunction $a$: definition (Schwartz, $\widehat a = a$), modular-form integral representation, explicit formula, and boundary values $a(0)$, $a(\sqrt 2)$, $a'(\sqrt 2)$.

eigenfunction_b.description: The $-1$-Fourier-eigenfunction $b$: definition ($\widehat b = -b$), modular-form integral representation, explicit formula, and boundary values.

ElkiesCohn.title: New upper bounds on sphere packings I (Cohn–Elkies)

ConwaySloane.title: Conway–Sloane — Sphere Packings, Lattices and Groups
